column.name: record_type
column.description: Identifies the type of record represented by the row, such as an order transaction or a summary/target record. Analysts should use it to distinguish detailed sales rows from KPI or planning rows when aggregating.

column.name: division
column.description: Business division to which the record belongs; in this table values are typically NE. Use this for filtering or comparing performance by organizational division.

column.name: segment
column.description: Sales segment for the record, typically Retail in this view. Analysts should interpret metrics in the context of retail outlet sales rather than wholesale or other channels.

column.name: order_date
column.description: Date and time when the order was placed or captured. This is the transaction date for daily sales analysis and should be used for order-level time series reporting.

column.name: business_month
column.description: Business reporting month in YYYY-MM format, derived from the transaction or reporting calendar. Use it for monthly aggregation, target alignment, and month-to-date comparisons.

column.name: report_date
column.description: Date for which the row is reported in dashboards or MIS outputs. This may match the order date for transaction rows and should be used for report snapshot-based analysis.

column.name: uuid
column.description: Composite unique identifier for the field employee or position, often combining position code, employee name, and mobile/identifier details. Use cautiously as a business key because it may contain concatenated text rather than a system-generated UUID.

column.name: position_code
column.description: Sales position or territory code assigned to the employee, such as NER_GUJ_FF5. This is useful for territory-level reporting and mapping employees to routes or markets.

column.name: emp_id
column.description: Internal employee identifier for the salesperson or field resource associated with the record. Analysts should use it as the primary employee key where available.

column.name: emp_name
column.description: Name of the employee responsible for the outlet/order or metric. Use for display and reporting, but prefer emp_id or position_code for joins and deduplication.

column.name: designation
column.description: Job title of the employee, such as TSO or Sr. TSI. This supports hierarchy and role-based performance analysis.

column.name: tl_name
column.description: Name of the employee's team lead or immediate manager. Use it for rollups at team-lead level and supervisory performance reporting.

column.name: tl_designation
column.description: Designation of the team lead, such as ASM or BDM. This provides context on the management layer supervising the field employee.

column.name: rsm_name
column.description: Name of the Regional Sales Manager responsible for the employee or territory. Use it for regional hierarchy rollups and senior sales reporting.

column.name: rsm_designation
column.description: Designation of the regional manager, typically RSM. This helps identify the role level in the sales hierarchy.

column.name: asm_name
column.description: Name of the Area Sales Manager or equivalent manager mapped to the employee. Use for area-level aggregation and accountability reporting.

column.name: asm_designation
column.description: Designation of the ASM or equivalent manager, such as ASM or BDM. This describes the managerial role attached to the area hierarchy.

column.name: emp_zone
column.description: Sales zone assigned to the employee, such as West. Use it for geographic hierarchy reporting from employee territory perspective.

column.name: emp_state
column.description: State or state cluster assigned to the employee, such as Gujarat, Mah-Mum, or Mah-ABD. This reflects the employee territory and may differ from the outlet's state in cross-boundary cases.

column.name: emp_city
column.description: City associated with the employee's base or assigned territory. Use it to analyze field force deployment and city-level sales ownership.

column.name: hq_state
column.description: Headquarters state or HQ cluster for the employee. This is useful for aligning employees to their official HQ location rather than outlet location.

column.name: emp_type
column.description: Employee classification or employment type when available. Values may be null in transaction rows, so analysts should validate completeness before using it for segmentation.

column.name: state_group
column.description: Higher-level grouping of states used for business reporting when available. Null values indicate no grouping was populated for the row.

column.name: is_active
column.description: Flag indicating whether the employee is active in the organization or sales system. Null values should be treated as unknown rather than inactive unless business rules specify otherwise.

column.name: is_field_employee
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the employee is part of the field sales force. True values identify frontline resources responsible for outlet coverage and orders.

column.name: deactive_date
column.description: Date on which the employee was deactivated, if applicable. Null indicates no deactivation date is recorded or the employee may still be active.

column.name: client_id
column.description: Unique identifier for the client or outlet, such as a retailer code. Use this as the preferred key for outlet-level joins and counting unique stores.

column.name: client_name
column.description: Name of the retail outlet or client, such as om medical or nehal Beauty. Use for display and validation, but not as a unique key due to spelling and casing variations.

column.name: client_type
column.description: Type of client in the sales channel, typically Retailer in this view. Use it to filter or compare customer classes.

column.name: contact_type
column.description: Outlet contact or trade classification, such as Chemist, Cosmetic, or Retail-General Merchants. This supports channel mix and outlet type analysis.

column.name: client_zone
column.description: Geographic sales zone of the client or outlet. It may align with emp_zone but should be interpreted as outlet location context.

column.name: client_state
column.description: State or state cluster where the client/outlet is located. Use this for market-level sales reporting by outlet geography.

column.name: client_city
column.description: City where the client/outlet is located. This supports city-level demand, coverage, and distributor servicing analysis.

column.name: is_in_universe
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the outlet is part of the defined sales universe or targetable outlet base. True outlets are generally included in coverage and productivity denominator calculations.

column.name: is_new_outlet
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the outlet is newly added or newly activated during the reporting period. Use this for new outlet expansion and onboarding analysis.

column.name: product_id
column.description: Internal product identifier for the ordered item. Use it as the product-level key for joins to product master data.

column.name: variant_id
column.description: Internal identifier for the specific SKU variant. This is useful when one product has multiple sizes, packs, or formulations.

column.name: product_code
column.description: Business or ERP product code for the SKU. Values may appear numeric with decimal formatting, so analysts should treat it as a code/string rather than a measure.

column.name: vsku_code
column.description: Variant SKU code used for SKU-level sales tracking. It may match product_code in many cases and should be used for variant-level reporting where required.

column.name: product_name
column.description: Name of the product sold, including pack size or formulation. Use for readable SKU reporting and product performance analysis.

column.name: variant_name
column.description: Name of the specific product variant sold, usually including size or pack details. This is the most descriptive SKU label for variant-level reporting.

column.name: category
column.description: Broad product category, such as Bleach, HRC, Facewash, Gels, or Facial Kit. Use it for category-level sales and assortment analysis.

column.name: subcategory
column.description: More granular product family under the category, such as Gold Bleach or Neem FW. This supports deeper product mix analysis within categories.

column.name: product_category
column.description: Business-defined product category or display name, often combining product line and pack size. Use it as an alternate product grouping or reporting label.

column.name: mrp
column.description: Maximum Retail Price of the SKU, typically stored as a numeric value. Analysts should interpret it as consumer-facing price, not necessarily realized sales value.

column.name: is_npd
column.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the product is a new product development/new product launch item. Use it to track performance of newly launched SKUs versus established products.

column.name: distributer_name
column.description: Name of the distributor servicing the outlet/order. Use for distributor-level sales, coverage, and fulfillment analysis; note the column name uses 'distributer' spelling.

column.name: distributer_type
column.description: Type of distributor or channel partner, such as Distributor. This helps distinguish distributor classes in channel reporting.

column.name: source_name
column.description: Name of an alternate order source or upstream entity when available. Null values indicate no source was populated for the record.

column.name: source_type
column.description: Type/classification of the order source when available. Use with source_name to analyze whether orders came through specific systems, partners, or channels.

column.name: ss_id
column.description: Identifier for the super stockist or secondary sales entity when applicable. Null indicates no super stockist mapping is present for the row.

column.name: beat_name
column.description: Sales beat or route name associated with the outlet, such as Khokhara or Station Road Dombivali. Use it for route-level coverage, productivity, and outlet servicing analysis.

column.name: quantity
column.description: Number of units or selling quantity ordered for the SKU. Aggregate this for volume analysis, ensuring SKU pack-size differences are considered where relevant.

column.name: gross_value
column.description: Gross sales value of the ordered quantity before certain deductions or net sales adjustments. Use it for top-line value analysis but prefer NSV fields for net realization where required.

column.name: p3_val
column.description: Sales value attributed to the P3 bucket or metric layer for the row. In order rows it commonly mirrors gross order value and is used for P3 performance tracking.

column.name: p3_nsv
column.description: Net sales value attributed to the P3 bucket after applicable deductions or conversion factors. Use this for P3 achievement and revenue analysis on a net basis.

column.name: p2_val
column.description: Sales value attributed to the P2 bucket or metric layer. Values may be zero for rows not qualifying under P2 criteria.

column.name: p2_nsv
column.description: Net sales value attributed to the P2 bucket. Use it for P2 target achievement and net performance reporting where the P2 framework applies.

column.name: tc
column.description: Total call indicator/count for the row, generally showing whether an outlet call occurred. Analysts should aggregate carefully to avoid double counting across multiple SKU lines for the same outlet visit.

column.name: pc
column.description: Productive call indicator/count, usually 1 when the outlet/order qualifies as productive and 0 otherwise. Because order rows can contain multiple SKUs, deduplication by outlet/date/employee may be needed for true call counts.

column.name: tlsd
column.description: Total lines sold indicator/count for sales line productivity reporting. It typically marks SKU lines contributing to line sales and should be interpreted at line-item granularity.

column.name: ulsd
column.description: Unique lines sold indicator/count used to measure unique SKU or line contribution within an order or visit. Aggregate with care when multiple rows belong to the same order/outlet.

column.name: eco
column.description: Effective coverage or effective call indicator, typically 1 when the outlet contributes to effective sales coverage. Use it for coverage productivity, but avoid double counting across SKU lines if the metric is outlet-level.

column.name: mtd_tc
column.description: Month-to-date total calls for the relevant employee, outlet, territory, or reporting grain. Null on transaction rows means the MTD KPI was not populated for that row.

column.name: mtd_pc
column.description: Month-to-date productive calls for the relevant reporting grain. Use for monthly productivity tracking and compare with pc_target where available.

column.name: mtd_tlsd
column.description: Month-to-date total lines sold. This supports tracking of SKU-line productivity over the business month.

column.name: mtd_ulsd
column.description: Month-to-date unique lines sold. Use it to monitor breadth of assortment sold during the month.

column.name: mtd_eco
column.description: Month-to-date effective coverage count. This is used to assess how much of the outlet universe has been effectively covered in the month.

column.name: mtd_p3_nsv
column.description: Month-to-date P3 net sales value. Use it for tracking P3 progress against monthly P3 NSV targets.

column.name: mtd_p2_nsv
column.description: Month-to-date P2 net sales value. Use it for tracking P2 progress against monthly P2 NSV targets.

column.name: universe
column.description: Count or size of the eligible outlet universe for the relevant employee/territory/month. It is typically used as the denominator for coverage and productivity metrics.

column.name: lppc
column.description: Lines per productive call, a productivity metric showing average lines or SKUs sold per productive call. Higher values generally indicate better assortment penetration per successful visit.

column.name: productivity_pct
column.description: Productivity percentage, usually measuring productive calls or effective outcomes relative to total calls or universe. Analysts should confirm the exact denominator before comparing across teams.

column.name: pc_target
column.description: Monthly or period target for productive calls. Use it to calculate call productivity achievement and compare against mtd_pc or actual pc counts.

column.name: lppc_target
column.description: Target lines per productive call for the period. Compare actual lppc against this value to assess assortment productivity performance.

column.name: p3_target_nsv
column.description: Target net sales value for the P3 metric/bucket for the period. Use it with mtd_p3_nsv or p3_nsv to calculate P3 achievement.

column.name: p2_target_nsv
column.description: Target net sales value for the P2 metric/bucket for the period. Use it with mtd_p2_nsv or p2_nsv to calculate P2 achievement.

column.name: focus_target_vol
column.description: Target volume for focus products or focus initiatives. Use this for monitoring performance on prioritized SKUs/categories rather than total portfolio volume.

column.name: focus_target_eco
column.description: Target effective coverage for focus products or campaigns. It indicates the expected number of effective outlets or calls for focus execution.

column.name: pc_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement against productive call target. Values should be interpreted as actual productive calls divided by pc_target, subject to business calculation rules.

column.name: p3_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement against the P3 NSV target. Use it to compare P3 performance across employees, territories, and months.

column.name: p2_achievement_pct
column.description: Percentage achievement against the P2 NSV target. Use it for P2 performance tracking and incentive or review reporting where applicable.

column.name: new_outlet_count
column.description: Count of new outlets added or activated in the reporting period. Use it for outlet expansion analysis and validate against is_new_outlet for row-level detail.

column.name: new_outlet_p3_nsv
column.description: P3 net sales value generated from new outlets. This helps measure revenue contribution from newly added outlets during the period.
